post_text: I’ve always been a little hesitant about photoshoots, mainly because I’m not a fan of heavy makeup or over-styled looks. I usually prefer doing my own hair, makeup and outfits because I like keeping things natural, dewy, fast and easy-breezy. ✨ But this time was different. For this festive campaign with @meralda.jewels, I got to work with the most amazing team and honestly, they changed my mind about collaborative shoots completely. 🩷 Let’s start with my MUA @blee_by_karthikarai she nailed my brief to perfection. I told her I wanted something natural and skin-like, and she delivered exactly that in under 30 minutes! I didn’t even want to take my makeup off later 😅 My hairstylist too was on point soft, minimal styling just how I like it. @glamby_anuu 💁🏻‍♀️ And then came the showstopper the jewellery from @meraldajewels. Every piece was unique, especially the wristlet that goes over the back of the palm, I’ve genuinely never seen something like that before! Their collection truly stands out from anything I’ve worn before. ✨ Huge shoutout to the videographer @_mr.photoholic for bringing it all alive so beautifully. You can literally see the magic in every frame. And this stunning outfit from the most talented designer @shlokasudhakarofficial felt like it was made for me. The detailing, the embroidery, the fit… just perfect. 🤍 But above all, a massive thank you to my stylist @nidhisha_suvarna @style_affair_by_nidhisha , without whom none of this would’ve come together. She’s the reason this entire team collaborated, and her vision brought everything to life so seamlessly. Couldn’t have achieved this look without every single person on the team from hair, makeup, jewellery, outfit, and creative direction all working in perfect sync. 🩵 Here’s to a festive season filled with creativity, collaboration, and a little sparkle ✨ Happy Diwali, everyone!

5 years ago our life looked very different. Sagar was organising events and we were constantly out weekday parties, weekend parties, late nights that turned into late mornings, and hangovers that sometimes lasted two days ( longer than the actual party itself 😅 ) It was fun, it really was. But somewhere along the way we realised we were waking up tired, feeling unhealthy, fighting more, and honestly… not even remembering half the nights we called “the best nights of our life”. I finally understood what people mean when they say life changes after marriage. It’s not that you stop having fun. It’s that your idea of fun slowly changes. Today we still meet friends, still celebrate, still enjoy the occasional night out. But now we also choose slow mornings, work we’re proud of, better health, and a future we’re building together. Somewhere around turning 30, our bodies also made the decision very clear for us 😄 even the thought of a hangover now feels like YAPPPAAAHHH NAMASKARA 🙏🏻 And now waking up at 7am for yoga by a pond feels more like us than anything else ever did. Here’s to new versions of ourselves… and choosing sunrise over hangovers (most days) 😉 📍 @coorgmarriottresortandspa [ Healing, Adulting, Marriage Tips, Millennials, Growing Up, Morning Person, Night Owls, Wellness, Mindful Living ]

Recently, I heard something beautiful from our @thetemplegirl She described our bhoomi Tulunadu, Dakshina Kannada as strange. Strange… because no matter where life takes us, people from this land somehow find their way forward. Somehow, we are protected. Somehow, we rise. Growing up in Tulunadu, visiting Daivas wasn’t something extraordinary. It was normal. It was home. It was faith you didn’t have to explain, only feel. For centuries, these protectors have stood with our people. Through storms we spoke about… and storms we never even saw coming. And honestly, I truly understood what she meant only after moving away from Mangalore. That’s when I realised the value of what we grew up around. From childhood, we have walked into ancient temples, witnessed Daivas and stood in powerful consecrated spaces… not even knowing how deeply they were shaping us from within. Maybe that’s why there is a quiet strength within us. A strange courage. A strange reassurance that we carry wherever we go. She also said something that stayed with me, whoever has drank water from this land, whoever has roots here, will never truly lose in life. Wherever they go, whatever they do, they will find their way forward. And I felt that deeply. Because there is something special about this land. Something words cannot fully explain. It is meant to be felt. Not everyone will understand this feeling, but our Tulunadu people know it. No matter what you believe in this land holds space for you. And maybe that’s the real blessing of South Canara. These are our protectors. This is our identity. This is our feeling. This is our home. ❤️✨ Proud to be a daughter of Tulunadu. 🙏🏻

My mom banned me from the kitchen… so I did THIS 😭 So obviously… certified jugaadu mode ON 😌 Made my sleepover-famous secret Maggi using my steamer setup… and before you judge — YES, I ADD MILK TO MY MAGGI. Everyone who’s tried it? NEVER complained 👀 So here’s me not gatekeeping anymore 👇 ✨ MY SECRET MAGGI RECIPE ✨ • 1 glass water • 1 pack Maggi noodles + 2 masala sachets • A tea spoon of chilli powder • A tea spoon of coriander powder • A pinch of haldi • Salt (as per taste) • ½ tsp ghee • A splash of milk (THE SECRET 🤫 for that creamy, almost cheesy texture) ➡️ Boil, mix, let it get slightly creamy — DONE. Also steamed veggies on top because we pretend to be healthy 🥦✨ Gas saved. Mom happy. Me happier. PSIT कैंपस में दर्दनाक हादसा: जेसीबी से टकराकर BCA छात्र की मौत कानपुर नगर के सचेंडी थाना क्षेत्र स्थित PSIT कॉलेज कैंपस में निर्माण कार्य के दौरान एक बड़ा हादसा हो गया। BCA फाइनल ईयर के 21 वर्षीय छात्र प्रखर सिंह की जेसीबी से टकराने के बाद मौत हो गई। जानकारी के अनुसार, सोमवार शाम करीब 5 बजे छुट्टी के बाद प्रखर बाइक से कॉलेज के पीछे वाले गेट से बाहर निकल रहे थे। इसी दौरान एक तेज रफ्तार कार से उड़ी धूल के कारण आगे खड़ी जेसीबी उन्हें दिखाई नहीं दी और उनकी बाइक सीधी उससे टकरा गई। गंभीर हालत में पहले उन्हें कल्याणपुर स्थित BMC अस्पताल ले जाया गया, जहां से रेफर कर सर्वोदय नगर के रीजेंसी अस्पताल भेजा गया। देर रात इलाज के दौरान डॉक्टरों ने उन्हें मृत घोषित कर दिया। परिजनों ने आरोप लगाया है कि कॉलेज परिसर में बिना पर्याप्त सुरक्षा इंतजाम के निर्माण कार्य कराया जा रहा था। पुलिस मामले की जांच कर रही है। #kanpur #protest #psit #kanpurnews #psitkanpur
